goals.description:
Why it matters: drop from 28% to ~22% body fat this quarter — first third of the Musashi journey.
80% version: lose 2kg, gym 4x/week consistent, Sunday meal prep locked in.
Science: 2.4g protein per kg bodyweight (240g/day at 100kg) preserves muscle during fat loss (2025 research). 500 cal deficit = 0.5kg fat/week. Spread protein across 4-5 meals for maximum muscle protein synthesis.
Biggest constraint: junk food in the house, skipping Sunday prep, no tracking.
goals.title: April cut — lose 2kg: 240g protein/day, 500 cal deficit, gym 4x/week

goals.description:
Why it matters: month 2 of the cut — building momentum and proving the system works.
80% version: 90kg scale weight, Sunday meal prep 100% of weeks, no junk food in the house.
Science: if losing less than 0.3kg/week reduce calories by 200. If losing more than 1kg/week add 200 calories to protect muscle. Track the trend not single readings — weight fluctuates 1-2kg daily from water.
Biggest constraint: losing consistency after the first month.
goals.title: May cut — lose 2kg: 90kg target weight, meal prep every Sunday without fail

goals.description:
Why it matters: 3 months of consistency creates the habit loop — by June gym and meal prep are automatic.
80% version: 88kg scale weight, first visible muscle definition, habits automatic.
If stalling: reduce refined carbs further and add 1x30 min walk per day — burns 200 extra calories without affecting gym recovery.
Biggest constraint: summer social eating, losing focus in month 3.
goals.title: June cut — lose 2kg: 88kg target, first visible ab definition

goals.description:
Why it matters: your niche is already in front of you — Claude automation for property developers. You are learning both. Build the demo that proves it.
80% version: working feasibility calculator demo, shown to 5 property developers, feedback collected.
Market: AI consulting grew to $11B in 2025 at 26% CAGR — property dev is underserved and has high pain.
Biggest constraint: trying to build something perfect instead of shipping something real.
goals.title: April Claude — build property dev feasibility calculator demo using Claude

goals.description:
Why it matters: first paid client changes everything psychologically — it proves the market exists.
80% version: 1 paying client, minimum $500, payment received before work starts.
Offer: Claude Workflow Audit — 2 hours, map their process, show 3 automations that save 5+ hours/week, deliver PDF summary. Price: $500-1000.
Rule: collect payment BEFORE delivering. Free clients don't value your time and never become real clients.
Biggest constraint: undercharging or doing free work hoping it leads to paid.
goals.title: May Claude — land first paying client: $500 minimum, charge before delivering

goals.description:
Why it matters: case studies are your strongest sales asset — they convert future prospects better than any pitch.
80% version: $2k cumulative revenue, 2 case studies published on LinkedIn.
Case study format: Problem (what was painful) — Solution (what Claude did) — Result (time saved, money made, errors reduced).
Biggest constraint: not documenting the work you do — every project is potential proof.
goals.title: June Claude — reach $2k total revenue and publish 2 case studies

goals.description:
Why it matters: getting through the course is the minimum — doing the assignments forces real understanding.
80% version: Modules 1 and 2 submitted, basic feasibility run on 1 real Melbourne site.
Fix for execution fear: set a done-enough standard. Imperfect action beats perfect inaction every time.
Biggest constraint: watching videos but not submitting assignments — learning without output is procrastination.
goals.title: April property — complete Modules 1+2 with all assignments submitted

goals.description:
Why it matters: running real numbers on real sites is where theory becomes skill.
80% version: 3 real Melbourne sites analyzed in a feasibility spreadsheet with go/no-go verdict on each.
Key: always add 15-20% construction contingency — Australian standard. Most beginners underestimate this and it kills projects.
Sources: Feasly.com.au has free feasibility templates. Sites on realcommercial.com.au and development-ready.com.au.
Biggest constraint: analyzing imaginary sites instead of real listed ones.
goals.title: May property — analyze 3 real Melbourne development sites with full feasibility numbers

goals.description:
Why it matters: no course teaches what a developer learns on a live project — you need to see it firsthand.
80% version: 1 half-day with an active developer, understanding their real workflow and biggest risks.
Offer: help them automate 1 workflow using Claude for free in exchange for showing you the project.
Ask: What takes the most manual time? What is the biggest risk right now? What do you wish you knew before your first deal?
Biggest constraint: not reaching out because it feels awkward.
goals.title: June property — shadow 1 active Melbourne developer for a half day

goals.description:
Why it matters: the Barefoot Investor system is the foundation — without it savings are accidental not automatic.
80% version: ING Savings Maximiser open, $1000 deposited, automatic payday transfer set up.
System: Barefoot buckets — Daily Expenses 60% + Splurge 10% + Smile 10% + Fire 20%. Pay yourself first — transfer before spending anything.
ING Savings Maximiser: currently one of Australia's highest interest rates (~5% p.a.).
Biggest constraint: spending the savings before transferring them.
goals.title: April finance — Barefoot system live: $1k in ING Savings Maximiser on autopay

goals.description:
Why it matters: investing in index funds is the wealth-building engine used by every Aussie FIRE achiever.
80% version: Vanguard account open, first $500 invested in VAS + VGS, automatic monthly purchase set up.
Allocation: 70% VAS (Vanguard Australian Shares ETF — ASX300) + 30% VGS (Vanguard International Shares ETF). Standard Aussie FIRE allocation.
FIRE number: annual expenses x 25 = your target nest egg. If spending $40k/year you need $1M invested.
Biggest constraint: waiting until there is more money to invest — even $200/month compounds significantly over 20 years.
goals.title: May finance — open Vanguard Personal Investor and buy first $500 of VAS 70% + VGS 30%

goals.description:
Why it matters: measuring net worth monthly is what makes financial progress real — not just theoretical.
80% version: net worth improving month over month, FIRE number calculated, positive trend confirmed.
Formula: total assets (savings + investments + super) minus total liabilities (all debts) = net worth.
FIRE number check: what are your annual expenses? Multiply by 25. That is your long-term target.
Biggest constraint: avoiding the numbers because they look bad — the only way to improve them is to see them.
goals.title: June finance — net worth review: calculate FIRE number, confirm positive direction from April

goals.description:
Why it matters: without a clear problem statement you will keep building things nobody asked for.
80% version: top 3 pain points documented from real conversations, 1-sentence problem statement written and tested on 3 people.
Format: I help [Melbourne property developers] [automate X painful task] using Claude AI so they can [save 10 hours/week].
Rule: do NOT build anything this month. Only validate.
Biggest constraint: wanting to build before you understand the problem well enough.
goals.title: April business — document top 3 pain points from 20 conversations and write problem statement

goals.description:
Why it matters: collecting payment before building is the rule that separates real businesses from side projects.
80% version: 1 person has paid $500-1000 for your solution before you write a single line of code.
Offer: Claude Workflow Audit — $500 for 2 hours. You map their process and show exactly where Claude saves them 5+ hours/week.
If nobody will pay $500: the pain is not real enough or your positioning is wrong. Go back to conversations.
Biggest constraint: being scared to charge money before having the thing built.
goals.title: May business — pre-sell 1 Claude service: collect $1000 before building anything

goals.description:
Why it matters: a testimonial and case study are worth 100 cold messages — they do the selling for you.
80% version: first project delivered, written testimonial collected, case study published on LinkedIn.
Testimonial rule: ask within 24 hours of delivery. Most people forget if you wait longer.
Case study format: Problem they had — What Claude solved — Measurable result (time saved, money made, errors removed).
Biggest constraint: finishing the project and moving on without documenting or collecting the proof.
goals.title: June business — deliver first project, collect testimonial, publish case study

tasks.title: TODAY: Take body measurements — weight, waist, chest, hips + 3 photos front/side/back
TODAY: Download MyFitnessPal and set calorie target to TDEE minus 500 (approx 2400 cal/day). Protein target: 240g
TODAY: Remove all junk food from the house — bin it or give it away. Replace with: chicken, eggs, rice, broccoli, oats
THIS WEEK: Buy a food scale and weigh every meal for 7 days — people underestimate portions by 40-50%
THIS WEEK: Block gym sessions in calendar: Push Monday, Pull Wednesday, Legs Friday, Upper Saturday
WEEKLY SUNDAY: Meal prep 14 portions before 6pm — chicken 180g + rice 150g + broccoli. Non-negotiable.
WEEKLY MONDAY: Weigh yourself before eating — log weight and compare to last week
PUSH SESSION: Bench press 4x6-10, OHP 3x8-12, incline DB 3x10-15, lateral raises 3x12-15, tricep pushdown 3x12-15
PULL SESSION: Deadlift 3x5, barbell rows 4x8-10, lat pulldown 3x10-12, face pulls 3x15-20, curls 3x10-12
LEGS SESSION: Squats 4x6-10, leg press 3x10-15, Romanian deadlift 3x10-12, walking lunges 3x12, calf raises 4x15-20
TODAY: Commit to niche for 90 days — write this down: I automate workflows for Melbourne property developers using Claude
THIS WEEK: Join Anthropic Discord + 1 property tech or AI community and introduce yourself
THIS WEEK: Research the 3 most painful manual tasks for property developers — search LinkedIn, forums, Reddit
Q1 END: Post first LinkedIn content — show something you built with Claude before Q1 closes April 1
WEEKLY: Post 1 LinkedIn piece — specific Claude workflow, specific result, specific industry. Under 300 words.
WEEKLY: Send 10 outreach messages to property developers on LinkedIn — track sent / replied / meeting / paid
TODAY: Confirm Module 1 content and block a 3-hour slot this week to start it — not plan, actually start
Q1 DEADLINE: Complete Module 1 and submit the assignment before April 1 — 9 days left
THIS WEEK: Find 1 property developer on LinkedIn — offer free Claude automation help in exchange for mentorship
WEEKLY: Research 1 development site on realcommercial.com.au — check zoning and run basic feasibility numbers
TODAY: List every debt — credit cards, loans, HECS, car — exact balances and interest rates
TODAY: List all monthly income and all monthly expenses — every dollar in and every dollar out
THIS WEEK: Open bank statement and cancel every unused subscription — go line by line tonight
THIS WEEK: Order Barefoot Investor by Scott Pape — Australian FIRE bible. Read 1 chapter per day until done.
WEEKLY SUNDAY: 5-minute spending review — open banking app and categorize this week's expenses before watching TV
TODAY: Message 5 property developers on LinkedIn — ask only about their biggest time wasters. No pitch.
THIS WEEK: Have 3 conversations with property developers — log their pain points verbatim in a spreadsheet
Q1 RULE: Do NOT build anything until someone verbally commits to pay $500 for your solution
WEEKLY: Send 10 cold outreach messages to property developers — track sent/replied/meeting/paid in spreadsheet
APRIL: Write your 1-sentence problem statement and put it on your LinkedIn bio, email signature, and Notion
